title: brassica campestris var. trilocularis
intro: Brassica campestris var. trilocularis, also known as three-seeded mustard, is a variety of the common mustard plant, characterized by its distinctive trilocular seeds. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Brassica campestris var. trilocularis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically to alleviate skin irritations and wounds.
